channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines curated from global sources

item.title: 🇸🇦🇹🇷🇦🇪 Saudi Arabia, Turkey and UAE propose new trade corridors bypassing Hormuz
item.description: Middle Eastern governments are reviving decades-old pipeline proposals and drawing up new rail-sea transport corridors as a structural shift away from dependence on vulnerable Persian Gulf shipping routes. The plan would route cargo from UAE and Oman ports through Saudi Arabia to Jordan, then to Egypt's Suez or Syrian ports.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇩🇪 Trump says US will cut troops in Germany "a lot further than 5,000" amid NATO tensions
item.description: President Trump announced the US will reduce its troop presence in Germany beyond the announced 5,000 withdrawal, escalating strains with NATO allies. Germany's defense minister said the drawdown was expected while Republican lawmakers expressed concern about sending the wrong signal to Putin.

item.title: 🇮🇷🇺🇸 Iran says "ball in US court" on diplomacy as senior military expects renewed conflict
item.description: Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister said Tehran is prepared for both diplomacy or continued confrontation, while a senior Iranian military commander warned that renewed conflict with the US is "likely." The statements come as Trump reviews a new 14-point Iranian peace proposal.

item.title: 🇮🇷⛪ Jailed Iranian Nobel laureate Narges Mohammadi at "very high risk" after hospitalization
item.description: Iranian rights lawyer and Nobel Peace Prize laureate Narges Mohammadi was urgently transferred to a hospital in Zanjan after a cardiac crisis and fainting. Her family says her health has declined due to a beating during her December arrest, and Iran's Intelligence Ministry is opposing her transfer to Tehran for treatment.

item.title: 🇮🇱🇱🇧 Israel strikes southern Lebanon as ceasefire violations continue
item.description: Israel carried out strikes across southern Lebanon on Saturday, killing several according to Lebanese state media. The Israeli military said it hit dozens of Hezbollah targets. Both sides continue to accuse each other of ceasefire violations, with Hezbollah claiming attacks on Israeli troops.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇹🇼 Taiwan president defiant begins Eswatini trip as China calls him "rat"
item.description: Taiwan's president began a trip to Eswatini, one of Taiwan's few remaining diplomatic allies, amid escalating Chinese pressure. China's foreign ministry referred to him as a "rat" crossing the street.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 US warns shippers they could face sanctions for paying Iran's Strait of Hormuz tolls
item.description: The US Treasury Department warned shipping companies that paying passage fees to Iran for transiting the Strait of Hormuz could result in sanctions violations. Iran has imposed tolls on vessels passing through the strategic waterway.

item.title: 🇩🇪🇺🇸 Trump auto tariff hike could cost Germany nearly $18 billion in output, institute says
item.description: Germany's DIW economic institute estimates that Trump's proposed 25% auto tariff increase could cost Germany up to 18 billion euros in economic output, dealing a significant blow to Europe's largest economy.

item.title: 🛢️🇮🇶 Iraq says oil output can recover within week once Hormuz crisis ends
item.description: Iraq's oil minister stated that the country's oil production and exports can return to normal levels within a week once the Strait of Hormuz crisis is resolved. Iraq has been significantly impacted by the disruption to Persian Gulf shipping.

item.title: 🇯🇵🛢️ Japan's Taiyo Oil receives cargo of oil from Russia's Sakhalin-2 project
item.description: Japan's Taiyo Oil confirmed receiving a cargo of crude oil from Russia's Sakhalin-2 project, highlighting continued energy ties between Japan and Russia despite global sanctions pressure.

item.title: 🇰🇭 Cambodia's opposition leader Kem Sokha convicted, sentenced to 27 years
item.description: Cambodia's most prominent opposition leader Kem Sokha was sentenced to 27 years in prison under house arrest, effectively ending any political challenge to Prime Minister Hun Sen's rule. Rights groups condemned the verdict as politically motivated.

item.title: ⚔️🇺🇸 CNN analysis: Iran war two months in, no clear winner
item.description: A CNN analysis examines the ongoing conflict between the US-Israel alliance and Iran, now in its second month with no clear resolution in sight. The report questions whether military force alone can achieve US objectives.

item.title: 🇺🇸💰 Iran war stasis brings US inflation expectations back to a boil
item.description: Uncertainty surrounding the Iran conflict has pushed US inflation expectations higher as oil prices remain volatile. The Federal Reserve faces mounting pressure as the economic impact of the crisis continues.

item.title: ⚔️🇺🇸 US Navy turns to AI firm Domino for options to counter Iranian mines
item.description: The US Navy has contracted AI firm Domino to develop solutions for countering Iranian naval mines in the Strait of Hormuz. The technology aims to accelerate mine detection and clearance operations in the strategic waterway.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇺🇸 China's gravity-detecting squid gets closer to spotting US nuclear submarines
item.description: Chinese scientists have developed a new gravity-detecting sensor system called the "squid" that could improve the detection of US nuclear submarines. The technology represents a significant advancement in anti-submarine warfare capabilities.

item.title: 🇨🇾🌊 BBC: Major offshore gas find in Cyprus sparks regional interest
item.description: A significant offshore natural gas discovery in Cyprus has drawn attention from regional energy players. The find could reshape Eastern Mediterranean energy dynamics.

item.title: 🇲🇱 Mali probes soldiers suspected of involvement in military base attacks
item.description: Mali's military government has launched an investigation into soldiers suspected of involvement in recent attacks on military bases, raising concerns about internal security threats.

item.title: 🇿🇲 Zambia cancels global mining summit amid rights concerns
item.description: Zambia cancelled the RightsCon 2026 summit, a major digital rights conference, citing concerns over potential protests. Rights groups criticized the decision as suppressing free speech.

item.title: 💊🌍 First malaria drug for babies approved
item.description: The World Health Organization has approved the first-ever malaria vaccine specifically designed for babies, a major breakthrough in the fight against the disease that kills hundreds of thousands of children annually.

item.title: 🇺🇦🇪🇺 Kyiv Independent: "Reliable partner replaced by transactional player" — US delays arms to Europe amid Iran war
item.description: European allies are facing delays in US arms shipments as Washington prioritizes its own military needs in the Iran conflict. The shift has raised concerns about the reliability of the US as a security partner.

item.title: 🇨🇿🇷🇺 Czechia allows Fico's Moscow flight as neighbors close airspace for Victory Day
item.description: Slovakia's Prime Minister Robert Fico will fly to Moscow for Russia's Victory Day celebrations. Czechia allowed the flight through its airspace while other European nations have closed their skies to Russian-connected flights.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Zelensky invites Fico to Kyiv ahead of Moscow Victory Day trip
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky invited Slovak Prime Minister Robert Fico to visit Kyiv before his planned trip to Moscow for Victory Day celebrations, in an attempt to dissuade him from the controversial visit.

item.title: 🇵🇪🇷🇺 Peru launches investigation into citizens trafficked to fight Russia's war in Ukraine
item.description: Peru has opened an investigation into reports that Peruvian citizens are being trafficked and recruited to fight for Russia in Ukraine, highlighting the international recruitment networks supporting Moscow's war effort.

item.title: 🇮🇷🇺🇸 Reuters: Iranian proposal rejected by Trump would open Strait before nuclear talks
item.description: Iran submitted a proposal that would open the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for a pause in hostilities and nuclear negotiations, but Trump rejected the offer. The proposal was delivered via Pakistani mediators.
